Hermes Menswear Creative Chief Veronique Nichanian to Leave After Nearly Four Decades
data.description:
By Andrea Figueras


Hermes said its artistic director of menswear, Veronique Nichanian, would step down after her last show for the French luxury house in January after 37 years at the company.

The maker of luxury Birkin bags said Friday that the designer's final collection would be presented at this season's Paris Fashion Week.

"Veronique Nichanian has written, with tremendous talent, the story of men's ready-to-wear at Hermes and has applied her vision across the entire men's universe," the company said Friday.

For nearly 40 years, Nichanian's collections have found strong and consistent success with clients all over the world, the group said. It didn't name a successor.

"Working for Hermes since 1988 has been an immense pleasure," Nichanian said. "My wish has always been to create clothes of today for the long term."

The move is the latest in a series of creative shifts across the luxury space as brands try to regain consumers' appeal with fresh designs amid a global downturn in demand for high-end goods. Labels including Christian Dior, Fendi, Gucci and Chanel have recently hired new designers.

Despite the slowdown, Hermes has been able to weather the storm, helped by its well-heeled customers, who have continued to indulge themselves with luxuries. By contrast, brands that target less affluent consumers have been harder hit.


Write to Andrea Figueras at andrea.figueras@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-17-25 0641ET

Dalmia Bharat Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Second Quarter and Six Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Dalmia Bharat Limited reported earnings results for the second quarter and six months ended September 30, 2025. For the second quarter, the company reported sales was INR 34,170 million compared to INR 30,870 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 34,830 million compared to INR 31,600 million a year ago. Net income was INR 2,360 million compared to INR 460 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 12.59 compared to INR 2.46 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 12.59 compared to INR 2.46 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was INR 12.59 compared to INR 2.45 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was INR 12.59 compared to INR 2.45 a year ago.
For the six months, sales was INR 70,530 million compared to INR 67,080 million a year ago. Revenue was INR 71,680 million compared to INR 68,310 million a year ago. Net income was INR 6,290 million compared to INR 1,870 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 33.53 compared to INR 9.96 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was INR 33.53 compared to INR 9.96 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was INR 33.54 compared to INR 9.97 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share was INR 33.54 compared to INR 9.97 a year ago.

Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Shares Fall as Trump Moves to Cut Price of Weight-Loss Drugs
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping


Novo Nordisk shares slumped Friday after President Trump said he would lower the price of the Danish company's Ozempic drug for Americans.

Speaking in the Oval Office Thursday, Trump said the cost of Ozempic, or "the fat loss drug," should be "much lower" when negotiations with Novo Nordisk have concluded.

Ozempic is approved to treat diabetes, but patients commonly use it for obesity as it shares the same active ingredient as its Wegovy weight-loss drug. The company sells both drugs in the U.S.

The administration is negotiating the price of drugs with several pharmaceutical companies, but talks with Novo Nordisk continue, and Trump said the cost of Ozempic could come down as low as $150.

Analysts at Sydbank said in a note that the comments caused the U.S. shares of Novo Nordisk to drop, as the touted price of $150 a month compares to the current list price of $1,000.

The company's Danish-listed shares were 6.2% lower in late morning trade.

A Novo Nordisk spokesperson said the company has engaged in discussions with the administration regarding the most favored nation executive order.

"We remain focused on improving patient access and affordability, and we will continue to work to find solutions that help people access the medication they need," the spokesperson said.

Trump's executive order seeks to institute the most favored nation policy, whereby the U.S. government pays prices for drugs that are tied to the prices paid by other countries.


Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-17-25 0625ET

Xiaomi shares post worst week in 3-1/2 years as accidents stoke EV safety concerns
data.description:
BEIJING (Reuters) -Shares of Chinese EV maker Xiaomi suffered their biggest weekly loss in more than 3-1/2 years on Friday, after media reported two separate accidents involving its best-selling model this week.

Xiaomi shares ended the day 3.7% lower at HK$45.96 ($5.92), the lowest close since April 22. For the week, they tumbled 11.7%, the worst such decline since late January 2022, though year-to-date, the shares are still up by a third.

On Friday morning a SU7 sedan lost control in the central city of Wuhan, where it knocked down guard rails in the middle of the road, drove into the opposite lane and collided with a van, according to the Sanxiang Metropolitan Daily, a government-backed newspaper.

Video from the scene circulated by the newspaper showed the car's left front wheel axle had broken. It was not clear if there were any casualties.

That followed an incident on Monday involving a 31-year-old man, suspected of drunk driving, who was killed in a collision in Chengdu. When passersby tried to pull him out of his burning SU7 Ultra they were unable to open the car doors, Chinese media reported.

Xiaomi hasn't publicly acknowledged that the accidents involved its electric vehicles. Chinese police issued a statement that detailed the Chengdu incident but did not name Xiaomi.

The company didn't respond to a request for comment.  

In a speech to an industry event in Beijing on Thursday, his first public appearance after the Chengdu accident, Xiaomi CEO Lei Jun made no mention of the crash, but called on the industry to focus resources and energy on technology R&D under the government's guidance and with safety as the basis. 

He also urged a joint boycott of online trolls and malicious negative campaigns.

The Chinese smartphone maker turned EV maker launched the SU7 in March 2024. The electric sedan had begun outselling Tesla's Model 3 on a monthly basis since December, before handing the lead back in September.

Under a product recall issued last month by the market regulator, Xiaomi announced a software update for over 115,000 SU7 sedans to fix potential safety issues when its assisted driving features are used.

It saw bumper orders for its electric SUV YU7 in late June, despite a deadly crash involving an SU7 in assisted driving mode at the end of March.  

($1 = 7.7668 Hong Kong dollars)

(Reporting by Beijing, Shanghai and Hong Kong newsrooms; Editing by David Dolan and Kim Coghill)
